channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Clinton Advocates Electoral System Overhaul in the U.S.
item.description: Former U.S. Secretary of State Hillary Clinton has renewed calls for significant reforms to the American presidential election system, emphasizing the need for a more representative process. Her advoc...

item.title: Delta CP Calls for Stronger Collaboration with Traditional Rulers on Security and Intelligence
item.description: The Commissioner of Police in Delta State, Mr. Yemi Oyeniyi, has called on traditional institutions across the state to continue strengthening their partnership with law enforcement agencies in order ...

item.title: Susanna Clarke reflects on illness, storytelling and the power of narrative
item.description: Susanna Clarke, author of 'The Invisible Library', shares her personal experience with chronic illness, drawing parallels between her journey and Virginia Woolf's essay. She explores how narratives sh...

item.title: President Ruto Criticizes MP Mukunji Over Political Allegiances and Development Focus
item.description: President William Ruto has publicly criticized Manyatta MP Gitonga Mukunji, accusing him of attempting to undermine his political authority and promoting divisive rhetoric instead of focusing on natio...

item.title: Senator Rodante Marcoleta Faces Plunder Charges in Anticorruption Court
item.description: Senator Rodante Marcoleta was arrested by the Sandiganbayan on July 6, 2026, following a non-bailable plunder case involving an alleged P75-million campaign donation. The court issued an arrest warran...

item.title: Border Matches in World Cup History: A Historical Overview
item.description: The article explores the frequency of border matches in FIFA World Cup history, highlighting Spain vs. Portugal as the first such encounter in 1930. It notes that 71 border matches have occurred acros...

item.title: Nuclear Regulatory Commission Proposes Easing Radiation Exposure Limits, Raising Health Concerns
item.description: Federal regulators are proposing to eliminate the ALARA standard, which aims to keep radiation exposure as low as reasonably achievable. This change would introduce a graded approach to radiation dose...

item.title: China to Conduct Nuclear-Capable Missile Test in South Pacific
item.description: China is preparing to test-fire a nuclear-capable long-range missile with a dummy warhead in the South Pacific within the next 24 hours, according to regional diplomats briefed by Chinese officials. T...

item.title: U.S. Ambassador Denies Any Contact Between Trump Administration and Alberta Separatist Groups
item.description: U.S. Ambassador to Canada Pete Hoekstra has firmly denied claims that the Trump administration is meeting with or strategizing alongside Alberta separatist groups, directly contradicting reports and a...

item.title: UNRWA Fails to Implement Hate Speech Reforms Despite UN Recommendations
item.description: A recent analysis by IMPACT-se reveals that the United Nations Relief and Works Agency (UNRWA) has failed to implement critical reforms recommended by the Colonna Report, an independent review group e...

item.title: Labor Party Needs Stronger Anti-Wealth Policies Amid Media Scrutiny
item.description: The article critiques the Australian Labor Party's lack of substantive anti-wealth policies despite media narratives framing them as anti-wealth. It highlights how conservative media consistently port...

item.title: Vincent Osiemo Dies After Falling From Vehicle During Political Rally in Kisii; Family Demands Justice
item.description: Vincent Osiemo, known as Mapinduzi, died after falling from a moving vehicle during a political rally in Kisii. The incident occurred when attackers threw stones at the convoy, forcing vehicles to spe...

item.title: EAC Kiswahili Commission Discusses AI's Role in Language Development
item.description: The third International Kiswahili Conference, held in Burundi from July 5, 2026, focused on integrating artificial intelligence (AI) to advance the Kiswahili language across East Africa. Dr. Caroline ...

item.title: Mumbai Monsoon Tragedy: Man Killed After Tree Falls on Shop, City Under Red Alert
item.description: Mumbai faced severe monsoon rains on Sunday, leading to tragic incidents and widespread disruptions. A 63-year-old man died after a tree collapsed on his shop in Kurla West, trapping him inside. The i...

item.title: Canadian swimmer Summer McIntosh sets 200m butterfly world record at trials
item.description: At the Canadian swimming trials in Montreal, 19-year-old phenom Summer McIntosh shattered the women’s 200-metre butterfly world record with a time of 2:01.65, breaking the previous mark set by Chinese...

item.title: Long-Short Strategies Offer Tax Efficiency Benefits for Investors
item.description: Long-short strategies, which combine long positions in stocks with short positions, provide tax efficiency benefits by offsetting capital gains with realized losses. This approach, explained by Jeff B...

item.title: Morocco advances in World Cup after decisive victory over Canada
item.description: Morocco's midfield maestro Azzedine Ounahi delivered a standout performance in their 3-0 World Cup round-of-16 victory over Canada, capitalizing on a tactical shift that placed him higher up the pitch...

item.title: UK Government Proposes Donation Caps for Overseas Voters and Stricter Company Donation Checks
item.description: The UK government has proposed new restrictions on political donations from overseas voters relocating to the country, including a £100,000 annual cap. These measures aim to prevent foreign influence ...

item.title: Tanzania's Religious Leader Announces Private Militia Amid Political Tensions
item.description: Sheikh Said Mwaipopo, a prominent Muslim cleric in Tanzania, has sparked intense debate by announcing his private militia, 'Black Tiger,' to combat anti-government protests. The group, composed of you...

item.title: NRC Proposes Eliminating ALARA Radiation Standard to Cut Costs
item.description: The U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) has proposed eliminating the ALARA (As Low as Reasonably Achievable) radiation protection standard, a move that could allow higher radiation exposure for w...
